items.title: US Dollar Index holds gains near 99.50 due to Iran conflict uncertainty
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the US Dollar (USD) against six major currencies, recovers its recent losses from the previous day and is trading around 99.40 during the early European hours on Tuesday.

items.title: Oil: Higher conflict-driven baseline holds – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities strategist Pooja Kumra argues that disruptions in Qatar and Iran justify a higher Oil price baseline for 2026 versus pre-crisis levels. The bank keeps its assumption of a mid‑$90s range and notes that even a 10% Oil rise can significantly lift headline inflation.

items.title: When are the German/ Eurozone flash HCOB PMIs and how could they affect EUR/USD?
items.summary: The preliminary German and Eurozone flash HCOB Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) data for March is due for release today at 08:30 and 09:00 GMT, respectively.

items.title: Fed: War fog clouds rate path – BNY
items.summary: BNY strategist John Velis says the March FOMC broadly matched expectations, with no rate change and minimal forward guidance, while markets swiftly repriced toward a more hawkish path.

items.title: JPY: Disinflation tempers energy shock – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank analyst Volkmar Baur highlights that Japanese inflation fell more than expected in February, with weak services and food prices pointing to ongoing disinflation despite higher Oil.

items.title: BoJ’s Ueda says to expect underlying inflation to accelerate moderately
items.summary: Bank of Japan (BoJ) Governor Kazuo Ueda said on Tuesday that he expects underlying inflation to accelerate moderately. Ueda added that he will guide monetary policy appropriately to stably achieve our inflation target accompanied by wage gains.

items.title: NZD/USD extends rejection slide from 200-day SMA; declines to 0.5825 amid firmer USD
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair continues with its struggle to make it through a technically significant 200-day Simple Moving Average (SMA) and attracts fresh sellers during the Asian session on Tuesday.

items.title: WTI rises to near $91.00 as Gulf states edge toward direct involvement in Iran conflict
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) oil price rebounds after registering over 9% losses in the previous day, trading around $91.00 per barrel during the Asian hours on Tuesday.

items.title: Australian Dollar underperforms as Iran pushes back hopes of peace talks
items.summary: The Australian Dollar (AUD) trades lower against its major currency peers, trading 0.6% down to near 0.6760 during the Asian trading session on Tuesday.

items.title: US Dollar Index (DXY) approaches mid-99.00s amid fading Iran de-escalation hopes
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which tracks the Greenback against a basket of currencies, regains positive traction following the previous day's downfall to a nearly two-week low and climbs to the 99.35 area during the Asian session on Tuesday.

items.title: Canadian Dollar softens amid Middle East tension
items.summary: The USD/CAD pair gathers strength to around 1.3750 during the Asian trading hours on Tuesday. Uncertainty and the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran continue to boost the US Dollar (USD) against the Canadian Dollar (CAD).

items.title: NZD/USD moves below 0.5850 amid increased risk aversion
items.summary: NZD/USD has pared its recent gains from the previous day, trading around 0.5830 during the Asian session on Tuesday. The pair depreciates as the US Dollar (USD) gains on increased risk aversion after the Israeli military said it had launched a fresh wave of strikes on Tehran.
